name: Veal Francaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Place veal cutlets between 2 sheets of wax paper; flatten to 1/4 inch thickness using a meat mallet or rolling pin (or have butcher cut veal for scallopine).
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Dredge in flour, then dip in beaten egg.
4. Dredge again in flour.
5. Heat 1/2 cup melted butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
6. Saute veal for 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove veal and reserve drippings in the skillet; set aside and keep warm.
7. Add chicken broth, wine, 1/4 cup melted butter, lemon juice (heavy cream if desired) to the skillet. Stir well. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring frequently.
8. Return reserved veal to the skillet. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
9. Remove veal mixture to a serving platter. Place lemon slices on or around the veal.
10. Sprinkle with minced parsley. Serve immediately.
